Package io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.wire

Class Encoding

java.lang.Object
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.wire.Encoding
public final class Encoding extends Object
The Zenoh Encoding field carried inside a Put payload.

Wire layout (from commons/zenoh-protocol/src/core/encoding.rs):

  7 6 5 4 3 2 1 0
 +-+-+-+-+-+-+-+-+
 ~   id: z16   |S~   varint (u16 id shifted left by 1 | S flag)
 +---------------+
 ~schema: <u8;z8>~   if S==1: length-prefixed byte schema (max 255 bytes)
 +---------------+

The prefix ID (14 bits effective — the codec caps at u16 so the composite id<<1 | S fits in a 17-bit varint) is a Zenoh-defined enum published as part of the API. The wire protocol itself doesn't care what the numbers mean. The optional schema suffix is arbitrary bytes (typically an ASCII MIME parameter).

An EMPTY constant matches the Rust Encoding::empty() value; Put encode omits the Encoding field entirely (and clears the E flag) when its value equals this.

    • EMPTY

    • ID_ZENOH_BYTES

      public static final int ID_ZENOH_BYTES
      Convenience prefix IDs. These match the values published in the Zenoh Java API but the wire protocol itself is agnostic.
